title: pseudognaphalium viscosum
intro: Pseudognaphalium viscosum, also known as sticky cudweed, is a plant species that belongs to the aster family and is native to the western United States and Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Pseudognaphalium viscosum include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce inflammation, and act as an antiseptic to prevent infection. The plant can be prepared in herbal teas, infusions, or salves, which can be used topically or taken orally to harness its medicinal properties.
